articles.headline: India Celebrates 76th Republic Day: A Testament to Constitutional Ethos and Democratic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India marks its 76th Republic Day with grand celebrations at Kartavya Path, showcasing its military might, cultural diversity, and economic progress.
• The parade featured advanced defense capabilities, tableaux from various states and ministries depicting 'Viksit Bharat' theme.
• The President addressed the nation, emphasizing the strength of the Constitution, democratic values, and the nation's journey towards becoming a developed economy by 2047.
• This year's celebrations underscore India's commitment to its founding principles of justice, liberty, equality, and fraternity, enshrined in its Constitution.
• A high-level delegation from a key Global South partner nation attended as the Chief Guest, reinforcing India's diplomatic outreach.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Republic Day is celebrated on January 26th each year, commemorating the adoption of the Constitution of India in 1950.
The President of India unfurls the national flag and takes the salute at the parade.
The parade showcases India's defense capabilities, cultural heritage, and socio-economic development.
The Preamble to the Constitution outlines the core ideals of the Indian Republic.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Republic Day in upholding India's constitutional values and democratic framework.
Analyze how the annual Republic Day parade reflects India's progress in defense, technology, and cultural preservation.
Evaluate the role of the Constitution in shaping India's socio-political landscape and its relevance in contemporary challenges.
Examine the concept of 'Viksit Bharat' as envisioned in national policies and its alignment with constitutional goals.
articles.imageDescription: President of India saluting during the Republic Day parade at Kartavya Path with the Chief Guest.
articles.trivia: On January 26, 1930, the Indian National Congress proclaimed 'Purna Swaraj' (complete self-rule), which is why this date was chosen for the Constitution's promulgation.

articles.headline: President's Republic Day Eve Address: Call for Unity and Resolve in Nation Building
articles.explainer:
• On the eve of Republic Day, the President of India addressed the nation, highlighting achievements and future aspirations.
• The address underscored the importance of national unity, social harmony, and collective efforts towards 'Viksit Bharat'.
• Emphasis was placed on inclusive growth, empowering women, youth, farmers, and marginalized sections of society.
• The President reiterated India's commitment to democratic principles, constitutional values, and global peace.
• Citizens were urged to uphold their fundamental duties and contribute actively to nation-building.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The President of India delivers an address to the nation on the eve of Republic Day.
The address typically covers national achievements, future goals, and calls for citizen participation.
Fundamental Duties are enshrined in Part IV-A of the Indian Constitution (Article 51A).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key themes of the President's address and their relevance to contemporary Indian society.
Discuss the significance of fundamental duties in fostering responsible citizenship and national development.
Evaluate the government's efforts towards inclusive growth and women's empowerment as highlighted in the address.
articles.imageDescription: President of India delivering the Republic Day eve address from Rashtrapati Bhavan.
articles.trivia: The first President to address the nation on Republic Day eve was Dr. Rajendra Prasad.

articles.headline: Padma Awards 2025 Announced: Honouring Exceptional Contributions Across Fields
articles.explainer:
• The Government of India announced the list of Padma Awards recipients for the year 2025 on the eve of Republic Day.
• The awards – Padma Vibhushan, Padma Bhushan, and Padma Shri – recognize distinguished and exceptional achievements in various disciplines.
• This year's list includes prominent personalities from art, literature, education, sports, medicine, social work, public affairs, science and engineering, trade and industry.
• The selection aims to honor unsung heroes and individuals who have made significant societal impact, reflecting India's diverse talent pool.
• The awards will be conferred by the President of India at ceremonial functions later in the year.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Padma Awards are one of the highest civilian honors of India.
They are announced annually on the eve of Republic Day.
There are three categories: Padma Vibhushan (for exceptional and distinguished service), Padma Bhushan (distinguished service of a high order), and Padma Shri (distinguished service in any field).
Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award, followed by the Padma Vibhushan, Padma Bhushan, and Padma Shri.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of civilian awards like the Padma Awards in promoting excellence and recognizing societal contributions.
Analyze the criteria and process of selection for these awards, and suggest measures to enhance transparency and inclusivity.
Evaluate the impact of such awards on motivating individuals and fostering a culture of public service and innovation.
articles.imageDescription: Illustrative image of the Padma Awards medallions.
articles.trivia: The Padma Awards were instituted in 1954, but were briefly suspended during 1977-1980 and 1992-1998.

articles.headline: India-Africa Growth Partnership Summit 2025 Concludes with Focus on Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The India-Africa Growth Partnership Summit, held in New Delhi, concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing sustainable development, trade, and capacity building.
• The summit saw participation from heads of state and government from numerous African nations, reinforcing India's commitment to South-South cooperation.
• Key agreements were signed in areas such as renewable energy, digital infrastructure, healthcare, and agricultural technology.
• Discussions focused on leveraging India's experience in digital public infrastructure (DPI) and affordable healthcare for African development.
• The partnership aims to address global challenges like climate change and food security through collaborative initiatives.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India has a long-standing partnership with African nations, primarily focused on capacity building, trade, and development assistance.
The 'India-Africa Forum Summit' (IAFS) is a platform for this engagement.
Key areas of cooperation include healthcare, education, agriculture, and infrastructure development.
India's 'Look Africa' policy emphasizes a demand-driven approach to partnership.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations and its significance for India's foreign policy objectives.
Discuss how India's development cooperation model, particularly in digital and renewable energy sectors, benefits African nations.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in deepening trade and investment ties between India and Africa.
Examine the role of India-Africa partnership in promoting the interests of the Global South on international platforms.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and African nations at the summit, shaking hands.
articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to advocate for the decolonization of African nations and has been a strong supporter of their sovereignty.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Privacy as Integral to Fundamental Rights in Landmark Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment, reaffirming that digital privacy is an intrinsic part of the fundamental right to privacy under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling came in a case challenging state surveillance and data collection practices without robust legal safeguards.
• The court emphasized the need for a balanced approach, protecting individual privacy while acknowledging legitimate state interests in security.
• It called for a comprehensive data protection framework that adheres to principles of necessity, proportionality, and accountability.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for data governance, digital services, and state-citizen interactions in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
Article 21 of the Constitution guarantees the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
India has been working on a comprehensive data protection law, based on the Srikrishna Committee report.
The concept of 'proportionality' is often applied by courts when balancing fundamental rights with state interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on digital privacy for individual liberties and government data practices.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security concerns with the fundamental right to privacy in the digital era.
Evaluate the adequacy of existing legal frameworks for data protection in India and suggest necessary reforms.
Examine how this judgment contributes to the evolution of fundamental rights jurisprudence in India.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a digital overlay representing data and privacy.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) explicitly recognized privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21, overturning earlier judgments that held otherwise.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Sees Significant Progress, Eyes Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• India's National Green Hydrogen Mission has shown promising progress, with several pilot projects achieving operational milestones.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Recent policy incentives and public-private partnerships are driving investments in electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen generation.
• The focus is on reducing production costs and developing robust supply chains for domestic and international markets.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its climate targets and transition towards a sustainable energy future.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission was launched to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
It aims to reduce reliance on fossil fuels and achieve net-zero emissions targets.
Electrolyzers are key equipment for green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Discuss the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform India's industrial landscape and contribute to global decarbonization efforts.
Suggest policy measures to further accelerate investment and innovation in the green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but 'green' hydrogen refers specifically to its production method using renewable energy.

articles.headline: India's Economic Outlook Robust Ahead of Budget: Pre-Budget Review Highlights Growth Drivers
articles.explainer:
• A pre-budget economic review indicates a robust economic outlook for India, driven by strong domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Key sectors like manufacturing, services, and infrastructure are showing sustained growth, contributing to employment generation.
• Inflation remains a monitored factor, with the Reserve Bank of India maintaining a cautious stance on monetary policy.
• The review highlights the resilience of the Indian economy amidst global headwinds, positioning it as a major growth engine.
• Upcoming Union Budget is expected to focus on further boosting investment, enhancing productivity, and ensuring fiscal prudence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The Union Budget is typically presented in February each year.
The Economic Survey, usually released a day before the budget, provides an overview of the economy.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rates to manage inflation.
Capital expenditure refers to government spending on creating assets like infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's economic resilience and growth in the current global scenario.
Discuss the challenges posed by inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the role of government capital expenditure and private investment in driving economic growth and employment.
Examine the expectations from the upcoming Union Budget in addressing economic priorities and achieving developmental goals.
articles.imageDescription: Graphs depicting economic growth and market trends.
articles.trivia: India's first Union Budget was presented on November 26, 1947, by the then Finance Minister R. K. Shanmukham Chetty.

articles.headline: Gaganyaan Mission Preparations Intensify: Focus on Crew Training and System Integration
articles.explainer:
• Preparations for India's ambitious Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission are intensifying, with significant progress in crew training and system integration.
• Astronaut candidates are undergoing advanced training modules, including simulated space environments and emergency procedures.
• ISRO is focusing on validating critical systems like the crew module, environmental control, and life support systems through rigorous ground tests.
• The mission aims to demonstrate India's capability to send humans to low Earth orbit and bring them back safely.
• This landmark mission is poised to elevate India's standing in global space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) is the nodal agency for the mission.
The mission aims to send astronauts into a Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
Astronauts are being trained in various facilities, including some abroad.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its geopolitical implications.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits and scientific advancements expected from the Gaganyaan mission.
Examine India's growing role in global space exploration and its collaborations with international partners.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
articles.trivia: If successful, India will become the fourth nation to independently launch humans into space.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Revitalize MSME Sector, Boost Exports
articles.explainer:
• The government introduced a new policy framework aimed at strengthening the Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector.
• The policy focuses on easier access to credit, technological upgradation, skill development, and market linkages, especially for exports.
• Measures include streamlined regulatory processes, enhanced credit guarantee schemes, and promotion of digital platforms for MSMEs.
• The goal is to integrate MSMEs into global supply chains and increase their contribution to India's GDP and employment.
• This initiative is critical for fostering entrepreneurship and achieving inclusive economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are categorized based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
The MSME sector is a significant contributor to India's GDP, employment, and exports.
Schemes like MUDRA Yojana, PMEGP, and CGTMSE support MSMEs.
The government's 'Make in India' initiative heavily relies on the MSME sector.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of the MSME sector for India's economic growth, employment generation, and social equity.
Discuss the key challenges faced by MSMEs in India, particularly regarding credit access, technology adoption, and market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new policy framework in addressing these challenges and boosting MSME competitiveness.
Suggest further policy interventions to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and enhance their export potential.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with modern machinery, representing the MSME sector.
articles.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and about 48% to exports.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Coastal Defences and Border Security Amid Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
articles.explainer:
• India announced enhanced measures to strengthen its coastal defences and overall border security, responding to evolving regional geopolitical dynamics.
• The focus includes deploying advanced surveillance technologies, increasing naval patrols, and upgrading infrastructure along maritime and land borders.
• Inter-agency coordination among various security forces (Navy, Coast Guard, BSF, ITBP) is being streamlined for quicker response times.
• New acquisition plans for maritime surveillance aircraft and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are underway.
• These steps aim to safeguard national interests, counter illicit activities, and ensure territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Navy and Coast Guard are primarily responsible for India's maritime security.
Border Security Force (BSF) guards India's land borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.
Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP) guards the border with China.
Multi-agency coordination centers (MACCs) facilitate intelligence sharing for coastal security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted challenges to India's border and coastal security in the contemporary geopolitical environment.
Discuss the measures being undertaken by India to enhance its surveillance capabilities and response mechanisms along its borders.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination in comprehensive border management and coastal defense.
Examine the role of technology in modernizing India's security forces and ensuring territorial integrity.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard patrol boat on the sea with a coastline in the background.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, making coastal security a complex and vital aspect of national defense.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation: Focus on Teacher Training and Digital Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education reviewed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, highlighting areas of focus.
• Key emphasis is being placed on comprehensive teacher training programs to align with new pedagogical approaches.
• Development of robust digital infrastructure and e-learning resources is crucial for equitable access to quality education.
• Challenges related to curriculum redesign, assessment reforms, and resource allocation were discussed.
• The goal is to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands and foster holistic development.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key recommendations include a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, vocational education, and multidisciplinary approach.
DIKSHA platform is a major digital initiative for teacher training and e-content.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Discuss the critical challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in teacher capacity building and digital divide.
Evaluate the role of digital infrastructure and e-learning platforms in achieving the goals of equitable and quality education under NEP 2020.
Suggest strategies to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure the holistic development of students as envisioned by the policy.
articles.imageDescription: Students learning in a modern classroom setting with digital tools.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives to Combat Food Security Risks
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture announced new initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across India.
• These programs focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and sustainable farming methods.
• Farmers are being provided with advanced weather advisories and access to climate-smart technologies.
• The goal is to enhance agricultural productivity, ensure food security, and reduce the vulnerability of farmers to climate change impacts.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to sustainable development and adaptation to a changing climate.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Climate change poses significant threats to agricultural productivity and food security.
Initiatives like National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA) aim to develop climate-resilient technologies.
Water management techniques like micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler) are crucial.
MSP (Minimum Support Price) is a government intervention to protect farmers' income.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of climate change on India's agricultural sector and its implications for food security.
Discuss the various strategies and technological interventions being implemented to promote climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and farmer awareness in adopting sustainable farming practices.
Examine how climate-resilient agriculture can contribute to farmers' income stability and overall rural development.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern irrigation systems under changing weather conditions.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top producers of several agricultural commodities, but a significant portion of its agriculture remains rain-fed.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Hailed as Global Model for Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is being recognized globally as a blueprint for inclusive growth.
• International organizations and several developing nations are exploring adopting elements of India's DPI stack to enhance financial inclusion and public service delivery.
• The interoperability and scalability of these platforms have significantly reduced transaction costs and increased access to government services.
• Discussions are underway for technical collaborations and knowledge sharing with countries interested in replicating India's success.
• This showcases India's leadership in digital transformation and its potential to contribute to global digital development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a secure cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI approach is often cited as an example of 'tech for good'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the components and functionality of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its role in achieving financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
Discuss how DPI can be leveraged to foster inclusive growth and empower citizens, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Evaluate the potential for India's DPI model to be adopted by other developing countries and its implications for global digital cooperation.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, security, and digital literacy in the widespread adoption of DPI.
articles.imageDescription: Illustrative image showing connected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 130 billion transactions in 2023, making it the world's largest real-time payment system.

articles.headline: National Clean Air Action Plan Intensifies Efforts in Urban Centers to Combat Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The National Clean Air Action Plan (NCAP) is intensifying its efforts in major urban centers to address escalating air pollution levels.
• New targets for particulate matter reduction have been set, along with stricter enforcement mechanisms for industrial emissions and vehicular pollution.
• Focus areas include promoting electric vehicles, improving public transport, managing construction and demolition waste, and controlling biomass burning.
• Collaboration with state governments and local bodies is crucial for effective implementation and monitoring.
• This renewed push aims to improve public health outcomes and enhance the quality of life in polluted cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: NCAP was launched in 2019 to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024.
Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the nodal agency for air quality monitoring.
The Air Quality Index (AQI) indicates the level of air pollution.
Major sources of air pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial emissions, construction dust, and stubble burning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major sources and health impacts of air pollution in Indian urban centers.
Discuss the key strategies and interventions under the National Clean Air Action Plan (NCAP) to combat air pollution.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of NCAP, including inter-state coordination and enforcement issues.
Suggest innovative solutions and policy reforms to achieve sustainable air quality improvements in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A city skyline shrouded in smog, with clean air initiatives symbolically represented.
articles.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the world's most polluted cities, particularly during the winter months.

articles.headline: Indigenous Defence Manufacturing Receives Boost with New Procurement Orders
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced fresh procurement orders for indigenously developed defense platforms and equipment.
• This move is part of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign imports and promote self-sufficiency in defense production.
• The orders include advanced artillery systems, surveillance drones, and naval vessels from Indian public and private sector companies.
• The emphasis is on fostering domestic research and development, technology transfer, and creating a robust defense industrial base.
• This initiative strengthens national security and creates significant employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: DRDO is India's premier agency for military research and development.
The 'Negative Import List' for defence items aims to boost indigenous production.
Defence Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs) and private players are involved in manufacturing.
Defence industrial corridors have been established to promote manufacturing clusters.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic imperative of indigenizing defence manufacturing for India's national security and economic growth.
Discuss the key initiatives and policy measures undertaken by the government to promote 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in the defence sector.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the domestic defence industry in terms of R&D, technology absorption, and global competitiveness.
Suggest measures to further accelerate the indigenization process and create a self-reliant defence ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Advanced military equipment manufactured in India, with an Indian flag in the background.
articles.trivia: India is among the top global arms importers, making indigenization a critical policy goal.

articles.headline: New Initiatives Launched to Preserve and Promote India's Rich Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced new schemes and projects aimed at the comprehensive preservation and promotion of India's diverse cultural heritage.
• These initiatives include digital archiving of ancient manuscripts, restoration of historical monuments, and support for traditional art forms and crafts.
• Community participation and public-private partnerships are being encouraged for sustainable heritage management.
• Focus is also on leveraging technology for virtual tours and immersive experiences to engage a wider audience.
• The goal is to safeguard India's invaluable cultural legacy for future generations and boost cultural tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
UNESCO recognizes several Indian sites and traditions as World Heritage Sites and Intangible Cultural Heritage.
The 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' initiative promotes cultural exchange between states.
Traditional art forms and crafts are crucial for preserving India's cultural diversity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's cultural heritage in shaping national identity and promoting social cohesion.
Discuss the major challenges in preserving and promoting India's diverse tangible and intangible cultural heritage.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in heritage management.
Suggest innovative approaches, including leveraging technology and community involvement, to ensure sustainable preservation of cultural assets.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian historical monuments and traditional art forms.
articles.trivia: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, the sixth-highest number in the world.

articles.headline: Urban Governance Reforms Underway to Enhance Efficiency of Smart Cities Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a series of urban governance reforms aimed at improving the efficiency and impact of the Smart Cities Mission.
• Reforms include decentralizing decision-making, enhancing municipal capacities, and fostering greater citizen participation in urban planning.
• The focus is on integrating technology-driven solutions with citizen-centric services to create sustainable and livable urban spaces.
• Challenges related to funding, project implementation delays, and inter-agency coordination are being addressed.
• These reforms are crucial for managing rapid urbanization and developing future-ready cities.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) granted constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
Urban planning involves various aspects like infrastructure, housing, and public services.
Decentralization aims to empower local governments for better service delivery.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in transforming India's urban landscape.
Discuss the critical challenges in urban governance, including financial constraints, capacity deficits, and coordination issues.
Evaluate the proposed urban governance reforms and their potential to enhance the effectiveness of urban development programs.
Examine the role of citizen participation and technology in creating sustainable and livable smart cities in India.
articles.imageDescription: A modern smart city with integrated infrastructure and green spaces.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making urban planning crucial.

articles.headline: India Boosts Exploration and Investment in Critical Minerals for Strategic Autonomy
articles.explainer:
• India is significantly increasing its efforts in the exploration and extraction of critical minerals, vital for its strategic autonomy and economic growth.
• The Ministry of Mines announced new policies to attract private investment and advanced technologies in critical mineral exploration.
• Focus minerals include Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements, essential for electric vehicles, renewable energy, and defense industries.
• International collaborations are being pursued to secure supply chains and diversify sourcing for these crucial resources.
• This move is aimed at reducing import dependency and building a self-reliant manufacturing ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are essential for modern technologies and economies, with high supply risk.
Lithium, Cobalt, and Nickel are key components of EV batteries.
Rare Earth Elements (REEs) are used in high-tech devices and defense applications.
India has recently identified a list of 30 critical minerals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, technological advancement, and national security.
Discuss the challenges in domestic exploration and extraction of critical minerals in India.
Evaluate the new policy initiatives by the government to attract investment and technology in the critical minerals sector.
Examine India's efforts to secure global supply chains for critical minerals through international partnerships and agreements.
articles.imageDescription: Various critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements displayed.
articles.trivia: China currently dominates the global supply chain for many critical minerals and their processing.

articles.headline: India Continues to Leverage G20 Legacy for Enhanced Global Cooperation and Multilateralism
articles.explainer:
• India is actively building upon its successful G20 presidency, advocating for enhanced global cooperation on critical issues like climate change, sustainable development, and global economic stability.
• Efforts are focused on ensuring the implementation of commitments made during India's presidency, particularly those related to the Global South.
• India is championing reforms in multilateral institutions to make them more representative and effective in addressing contemporary challenges.
• Discussions are ongoing with G20 member states to advance common agendas on digital public infrastructure, health, and inclusive growth.
• This strategic engagement reinforces India's role as a responsible global actor and a voice for developing nations.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to Republic Day in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26th January 1950.
2. The President of India unfurls the National Flag at the Republic Day parade.
3. The Purna Swaraj declaration was made on 26th January 1930.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26th November 1949, and it came into force on 26th January 1950. Statement 2 is correct. The President of India unfurls the National Flag and takes the salute at the Republic Day parade. Statement 3 is correct. The Indian National Congress proclaimed 'Purna Swaraj' (complete self-rule) on 26th January 1930, which is why this date was chosen for the Constitution's promulgation.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are considered components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)?
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. DigiLocker
4. National Green Hydrogen Mission
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), facilitating identity, payments, and document exchange respectively. The National Green Hydrogen Mission is an initiative related to renewable energy and is not a component of DPI.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020:
1. It replaced the National Policy on Education of 1968.
2. It recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure for school education.
3. It aims to achieve 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2030.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986. Statement 2 is correct. NEP 2020 recommends a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure. Statement 3 is incorrect. NEP 2020 aims to achieve 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035, not 100% by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes 'Green Hydrogen'?
(a) Hydrogen produced from natural gas with carbon capture and storage.
(b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
(c) Hydrogen produced from coal gasification.
(d) Hydrogen produced as a by-product of industrial chemical processes.
options: (a) Hydrogen produced from natural gas with carbon capture and storage.
(b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
(d) Hydrogen produced as a by-product of industrial chemical processes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green hydrogen specifically refers to hydrogen produced through the electrolysis of water, powered by renewable energy sources like solar or wind. This process results in zero greenhouse gas emissions.

prelimsMocks.question: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in which landmark judgment?
options: (a) Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
(b) Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
(c) K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
(d) Minerva Mills v. Union of India
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
prelimsMocks.explanation: In 2017, a nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court in Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India case unanimously affirmed that the Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.

• It called for a comprehensive data protection framework that adheres to principles of necessity, proportionality, and accountability.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for data governance, digital services, and state-citizen interactions in the digital age.

Electrolyzers are key equipment for green hydrogen production.

GS3.headline: India's Economic Outlook Robust Ahead of Budget: Pre-Budget Review Highlights Growth Drivers
GS3.explainer:
• A pre-budget economic review indicates a robust economic outlook for India, driven by strong domestic demand and government capital expenditure.
• Key sectors like manufacturing, services, and infrastructure are showing sustained growth, contributing to employment generation.
• Inflation remains a monitored factor, with the Reserve Bank of India maintaining a cautious stance on monetary policy.
• The review highlights the resilience of the Indian economy amidst global headwinds, positioning it as a major growth engine.
• Upcoming Union Budget is expected to focus on further boosting investment, enhancing productivity, and ensuring fiscal prudence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The Union Budget is typically presented in February each year.
The Economic Survey, usually released a day before the budget, provides an overview of the economy.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rates to manage inflation.
Capital expenditure refers to government spending on creating assets like infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's economic resilience and growth in the current global scenario.
Discuss the challenges posed by inflation and global economic uncertainties on India's growth trajectory.
Evaluate the role of government capital expenditure and private investment in driving economic growth and employment.
Examine the expectations from the upcoming Union Budget in addressing economic priorities and achieving developmental goals.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphs depicting economic growth and market trends.
GS3.trivia: India's first Union Budget was presented on November 26, 1947, by the then Finance Minister R. K. Shanmukham Chetty.

GS3.headline: Gaganyaan Mission Preparations Intensify: Focus on Crew Training and System Integration
GS3.explainer:
• Preparations for India's ambitious Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission are intensifying, with significant progress in crew training and system integration.
• Astronaut candidates are undergoing advanced training modules, including simulated space environments and emergency procedures.
• ISRO is focusing on validating critical systems like the crew module, environmental control, and life support systems through rigorous ground tests.
• The mission aims to demonstrate India's capability to send humans to low Earth orbit and bring them back safely.
• This landmark mission is poised to elevate India's standing in global space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) is the nodal agency for the mission.
The mission aims to send astronauts into a Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
Astronauts are being trained in various facilities, including some abroad.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its geopolitical implications.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits and scientific advancements expected from the Gaganyaan mission.
Examine India's growing role in global space exploration and its collaborations with international partners.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
GS3.trivia: If successful, India will become the fourth nation to independently launch humans into space.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Revitalize MSME Sector, Boost Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The government introduced a new policy framework aimed at strengthening the Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector.
• The policy focuses on easier access to credit, technological upgradation, skill development, and market linkages, especially for exports.
• Measures include streamlined regulatory processes, enhanced credit guarantee schemes, and promotion of digital platforms for MSMEs.
• The goal is to integrate MSMEs into global supply chains and increase their contribution to India's GDP and employment.
• This initiative is critical for fostering entrepreneurship and achieving inclusive economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: MSMEs are categorized based on investment in plant and machinery/equipment and turnover.
The MSME sector is a significant contributor to India's GDP, employment, and exports.
Schemes like MUDRA Yojana, PMEGP, and CGTMSE support MSMEs.
The government's 'Make in India' initiative heavily relies on the MSME sector.
mainsPoints: Analyze the importance of the MSME sector for India's economic growth, employment generation, and social equity.
Discuss the key challenges faced by MSMEs in India, particularly regarding credit access, technology adoption, and market access.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new policy framework in addressing these challenges and boosting MSME competitiveness.
Suggest further policy interventions to integrate Indian MSMEs into global value chains and enhance their export potential.
GS3.imageDescription: A factory floor with modern machinery, representing the MSME sector.
GS3.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and about 48% to exports.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Coastal Defences and Border Security Amid Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
GS3.explainer:
• India announced enhanced measures to strengthen its coastal defences and overall border security, responding to evolving regional geopolitical dynamics.
• The focus includes deploying advanced surveillance technologies, increasing naval patrols, and upgrading infrastructure along maritime and land borders.
• Inter-agency coordination among various security forces (Navy, Coast Guard, BSF, ITBP) is being streamlined for quicker response times.
• New acquisition plans for maritime surveillance aircraft and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are underway.
• These steps aim to safeguard national interests, counter illicit activities, and ensure territorial integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Navy and Coast Guard are primarily responsible for India's maritime security.
Border Security Force (BSF) guards India's land borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.
Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP) guards the border with China.
Multi-agency coordination centers (MACCs) facilitate intelligence sharing for coastal security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted challenges to India's border and coastal security in the contemporary geopolitical environment.
Discuss the measures being undertaken by India to enhance its surveillance capabilities and response mechanisms along its borders.
Evaluate the effectiveness of inter-agency coordination in comprehensive border management and coastal defense.
Examine the role of technology in modernizing India's security forces and ensuring territorial integrity.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard patrol boat on the sea with a coastline in the background.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, making coastal security a complex and vital aspect of national defense.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives to Combat Food Security Risks
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture announced new initiatives to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across India.
• These programs focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and sustainable farming methods.
• Farmers are being provided with advanced weather advisories and access to climate-smart technologies.
• The goal is to enhance agricultural productivity, ensure food security, and reduce the vulnerability of farmers to climate change impacts.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to sustainable development and adaptation to a changing climate.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Climate change poses significant threats to agricultural productivity and food security.
Initiatives like National Innovations in Climate Resilient Agriculture (NICRA) aim to develop climate-resilient technologies.
Water management techniques like micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler) are crucial.
MSP (Minimum Support Price) is a government intervention to protect farmers' income.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of climate change on India's agricultural sector and its implications for food security.
Discuss the various strategies and technological interventions being implemented to promote climate-resilient agriculture in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and farmer awareness in adopting sustainable farming practices.
Examine how climate-resilient agriculture can contribute to farmers' income stability and overall rural development.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with modern irrigation systems under changing weather conditions.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top producers of several agricultural commodities, but a significant portion of its agriculture remains rain-fed.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Hailed as Global Model for Inclusive Growth
GS3.explainer:
• India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is being recognized globally as a blueprint for inclusive growth.
• International organizations and several developing nations are exploring adopting elements of India's DPI stack to enhance financial inclusion and public service delivery.
• The interoperability and scalability of these platforms have significantly reduced transaction costs and increased access to government services.
• Discussions are underway for technical collaborations and knowledge sharing with countries interested in replicating India's success.
• This showcases India's leadership in digital transformation and its potential to contribute to global digital development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a secure cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
India's DPI approach is often cited as an example of 'tech for good'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the components and functionality of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its role in achieving financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery.
Discuss how DPI can be leveraged to foster inclusive growth and empower citizens, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Evaluate the potential for India's DPI model to be adopted by other developing countries and its implications for global digital cooperation.
Examine the challenges related to data privacy, security, and digital literacy in the widespread adoption of DPI.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustrative image showing connected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 130 billion transactions in 2023, making it the world's largest real-time payment system.

GS3.headline: National Clean Air Action Plan Intensifies Efforts in Urban Centers to Combat Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The National Clean Air Action Plan (NCAP) is intensifying its efforts in major urban centers to address escalating air pollution levels.
• New targets for particulate matter reduction have been set, along with stricter enforcement mechanisms for industrial emissions and vehicular pollution.
• Focus areas include promoting electric vehicles, improving public transport, managing construction and demolition waste, and controlling biomass burning.
• Collaboration with state governments and local bodies is crucial for effective implementation and monitoring.
• This renewed push aims to improve public health outcomes and enhance the quality of life in polluted cities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: NCAP was launched in 2019 to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024.
Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the nodal agency for air quality monitoring.
The Air Quality Index (AQI) indicates the level of air pollution.
Major sources of air pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial emissions, construction dust, and stubble burning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major sources and health impacts of air pollution in Indian urban centers.
Discuss the key strategies and interventions under the National Clean Air Action Plan (NCAP) to combat air pollution.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of NCAP, including inter-state coordination and enforcement issues.
Suggest innovative solutions and policy reforms to achieve sustainable air quality improvements in Indian cities.
GS3.imageDescription: A city skyline shrouded in smog, with clean air initiatives symbolically represented.
GS3.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the world's most polluted cities, particularly during the winter months.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Defence Manufacturing Receives Boost with New Procurement Orders
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced fresh procurement orders for indigenously developed defense platforms and equipment.
• This move is part of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign imports and promote self-sufficiency in defense production.
• The orders include advanced artillery systems, surveillance drones, and naval vessels from Indian public and private sector companies.
• The emphasis is on fostering domestic research and development, technology transfer, and creating a robust defense industrial base.
• This initiative strengthens national security and creates significant employment opportunities.

Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: DRDO is India's premier agency for military research and development.
The 'Negative Import List' for defence items aims to boost indigenous production.
Defence Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs) and private players are involved in manufacturing.
Defence industrial corridors have been established to promote manufacturing clusters.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic imperative of indigenizing defence manufacturing for India's national security and economic growth.
Discuss the key initiatives and policy measures undertaken by the government to promote 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in the defence sector.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the domestic defence industry in terms of R&D, technology absorption, and global competitiveness.
Suggest measures to further accelerate the indigenization process and create a self-reliant defence ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Advanced military equipment manufactured in India, with an Indian flag in the background.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top global arms importers, making indigenization a critical policy goal.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Exploration and Investment in Critical Minerals for Strategic Autonomy
GS3.explainer:
• India is significantly increasing its efforts in the exploration and extraction of critical minerals, vital for its strategic autonomy and economic growth.
• The Ministry of Mines announced new policies to attract private investment and advanced technologies in critical mineral exploration.
• Focus minerals include Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and Rare Earth Elements, essential for electric vehicles, renewable energy, and defense industries.
• International collaborations are being pursued to secure supply chains and diversify sourcing for these crucial resources.
• This move is aimed at reducing import dependency and building a self-reliant manufacturing ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are essential for modern technologies and economies, with high supply risk.
Lithium, Cobalt, and Nickel are key components of EV batteries.
Rare Earth Elements (REEs) are used in high-tech devices and defense applications.
India has recently identified a list of 30 critical minerals.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, technological advancement, and national security.
Discuss the challenges in domestic exploration and extraction of critical minerals in India.
Evaluate the new policy initiatives by the government to attract investment and technology in the critical minerals sector.
Examine India's efforts to secure global supply chains for critical minerals through international partnerships and agreements.
GS3.imageDescription: Various critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements displayed.
GS3.trivia: China currently dominates the global supply chain for many critical minerals and their processing.

GS3.headline: India Advances Towards Comprehensive AI Ethics Framework to Guide Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• India is making strides in developing a comprehensive Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics framework to guide responsible innovation and deployment of AI technologies.
• The framework aims to address concerns regarding data privacy, algorithmic bias, transparency, and accountability in AI systems.
• Consultations are ongoing with industry experts, academia, civil society, and international bodies to ensure a robust and future-proof policy.
• The goal is to harness the transformative potential of AI while mitigating associated risks and ensuring ethical use.
• This proactive approach positions India as a leader in establishing global standards for ethical AI governance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: AI refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines programmed to think like humans.
Algorithmic bias occurs when an algorithm produces unfair or discriminatory outcomes.
NITI Aayog has been instrumental in drafting India's national strategy for AI.
Ethical considerations are crucial for the widespread adoption and trust in AI systems.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ethical challenges and societal implications of rapidly advancing Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies.
Discuss the key principles and components that a comprehensive AI ethics framework should encompass.
Evaluate India's efforts in developing an AI ethics framework and its potential impact on fostering responsible innovation.
Examine the role of international cooperation in establishing global standards and norms for ethical AI governance.
GS3.imageDescription: A human hand interacting with an AI interface, symbolizing ethical interaction.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

GS3.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Expands Beyond Metros, Fueling Growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• India's vibrant startup ecosystem is witnessing significant expansion beyond traditional metropolitan centers, with increased activity in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• Government policies like Startup India, along with incubators and accelerators, are fostering entrepreneurship in smaller towns.
• This geographical diversification is driven by factors such as lower operational costs, access to local talent, and government support.
• Emerging sectors include Agri-tech, Health-tech, and Ed-tech, tailored to regional needs and challenges.
• This trend is contributing to balanced regional development and creating new job opportunities nationwide.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.

prelimsPoints: The Startup India initiative was launched in 2016 to support startups in India.
A 'Unicorn' is a privately held startup company valued at over $1 billion.
Incubators provide support to early-stage startups, while accelerators offer intensive programs for growth.
Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities are experiencing growth due to improved infrastructure and digital connectivity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the growth of India's startup ecosystem beyond metropolitan cities.
Discuss the role of government policies and institutional support in fostering entrepreneurship in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of this decentralized startup growth on regional development and employment generation.
Examine the challenges faced by startups in smaller cities and suggest measures to further strengthen their ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant co-working space in a non-metro city, showcasing young entrepreneurs.
GS3.trivia: India has the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, after the US and China.
